folio.label: 49r
folio.l: And namelicú there was , a gret College
Men clepen the Soler halle , of Caumbrygge
There was here whete , & eke here malt I g¼unde
¢ And on a day , it happed in a stounde
Seke lay the Maunciple , on a maladie
Men wenden wysly , that he shulde deye
For whicú this Millere , stal bothe mele & corn~
An hondred tymes more , than byfornö
For there bifornö , he stale but curteisly
But now he was a thef , outrageously
For whicú the wardeyn chide , & made fare
But æäof sette the Millere , nougút a tare
He craketh bost , and swor it was nat so
¢ Tha~ne were there ©onge , pore scoleres two
That dwelleden in the halle , of whicú I sey
Testyf they were , and lusty for to pley
And only for here myrthe , and reuelrye
Vp on the wardeyn , besily they crye
To ©eue he~ leue , but a litel stounde
To go to Mille , and sen here cornö I grounde
And hardily , they durst leyn here nekke
The Millere shuld nat stelen hem , half a pekke
Of corn~ , by sleigút , ne by force he~ reue
And at the last , the wardeyn ©af he~ leue
Ioún higút æat one , & Aleyn hygút that other
Of on tounö were they bornö , that higút Strother
Fer in the Nortú , I can nat telle where
This Aleyn maketh redy , al his gere
And on an hors , the sakke he caste a non
Forth goth Aleyn the Clerk , & also Ioún
With good swerde & bokeler , by here syde
Ioún knew the weye , hem nedeth no gyde
And at the last , the sakke doun he laytú
Aleyn spak first , al hayl Symond in faytú
How fares thy fair dougúter , and thy wyf
¢ Aleyn welcome q¶ Symkyn , be my lyf
And Ioún also , how now what do ©e here
By god q¶ Ioún , nede must , nede hatú no pere
Hym byhoues to serue hiö self , æ has na swayn
Or ellis he is a fool , as clerkes sayn
Oure Maunciple I hope , he wol be dede
Swa werkes ay , the wanges in his hede
And forthy is I come , & eke Alayn
To grynde oure cornö , and carye mele a gayn
I pray ©ow spede vs hethen , what ©e may
¢ It shal be do q¶ Symkyn , be my fay
